title: Recursive strip_cache_control_ttl walks entire request body unboundedly
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/proxy.rs:1216-1233
## Violation
strip_cache_control_ttl uses unbounded recursion to walk arbitrarily-deep JSON. A pathological request body with deeply-nested arrays or objects will overflow the stack — the function is reachable from proxy_anthropic_messages on any request through OAuth. Although users typically send well-formed bodies, this is a per-request DoS surface that has zero protection (no depth counter, no input size validation before parsing). serde_json::from_str does not bound JSON depth by default.
## Mandated refactor
Either (a) cap recursion depth (max 32) and emit warn! when hit, or (b) rewrite iteratively with an explicit Vec<&mut Value> work stack. Add a serde_json::Deserializer config with .recursion_limit(N) before parsing the body in proxy_anthropic_messages.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
